title: Linux Internals: How /proc/self/mem writes to unwritable memory (2021)
summary: An obscure quirk of the /proc/*/mem pseudofile is its “punch through” semantics. Writes performed through this file will succeed even if the destination virtual memory is marked unwritable. In fact, this behavior is intentional and actively used by projects such as the Julia JIT...

Would love feedback especially from teams running mixed-language stacks.
title: Show HN: Skir – like Protocol Buffer but better
summary: Skir is a declarative language for defining data types, constants, and APIs. Write your schema once in a .skir file and generate idiomatic, type-safe code in TypeScript, Python, Java, C++, and more. One YAML file. One command. Watch mode recompiles automatically.The generated cod...
